commentaries.title: # Bangladesh Fixed Income Weekly: Rates Rally Extends but Long-End Demand Remain
commentaries.body:
# Bangladesh Fixed Income Weekly: Rates Rally Extends but Long-End Demand Remains Fragile

**Week ending March 10, 2026**

Bangladesh government securities posted broad-based gains this week, with yields declining 8-11 basis points across both the bill and bond segments as the market prices in an increasingly dovish monetary trajectory. The 364-day T-bill cleared at 10.07%, down 11 basis points, with a healthy 1.75x bid-cover ratio, while the 91-day bill printed at 9.85% with the week's strongest coverage at 1.92x—signaling robust front-end demand and growing conviction that Bangladesh Bank's next move is a cut.

The bond segment told a more nuanced story. The 5-year BGTB cleared at 10.82% with a respectable 1.6x cover, but the 10-year auction revealed persistent duration hesitancy—a tepid 1.4x bid-cover ratio forced 10% devolvement onto primary dealers, even as the cutoff fell 10 basis points to 11.15%. With CPI still running at 9.13%, real return compression at the long end is clearly testing investor appetite.

The curve presents notable distortions. The 2-year yield at 11.95% sits sharply above the 5-year at 10.82%, creating a pronounced inversion in the belly that suggests either mispricing or acute term premium dislocation. Meanwhile, the 15-year at 12.68% trades well through where a smooth interpolation would place it relative to the 10-year and 20-year, steepening the ultra-long segment aggressively.

With the repo rate corridor still anchoring the front end and reserves steady at $21.2 billion despite a stable taka at 119.5, the macro backdrop supports further easing expectations. However, until devolvement clears and the belly inversion resolves, duration extension should remain selective. Watch the April inflation print—any break below 9% could catalyze the next leg of the rally.

commentaries.title: Yields Drift Lower as Liquidity Builds
commentaries.body: The Bangladesh government securities curve continued its gradual descent this week, with the belly of the curve (2Y-5Y) leading the rally. The 364-day T-bill cutoff fell 11bps to 10.07%, marking the sixth consecutive weekly decline and the longest streak since the pre-tightening era. The 91-day bill, the system's liquidity barometer, touched 9.85% — firmly below the repo rate floor for the first time in this cycle. Sunday's T-bill auction saw bid-cover ratios above 1.7x across tenors, while Tuesday's bond auction told a more nuanced story: the 5Y cleared comfortably at 1.6x, but the 10Y saw modest devolvement of 200 Cr. Banks remain comfortable extending to five years but hesitate at the long end. The 10Y-91D spread narrowed to 130bps from 135bps last week — a slow but persistent flattening that suggests the market is pricing in further BB cuts. With excess liquidity now at BDT 185,000 Cr and climbing, next week's T-bill auction should see continued compression. Watch the 20Y for signs that institutional buyers are finally extending duration.
keyPoints: 364D yield down 11bps to 10.07% — sixth consecutive weekly decline
91D at 9.85%, now below the repo rate floor
10Y saw 200 Cr devolvement (10%), long-end remains sticky
commentaries.curveShapeNote: Normal upward-sloping with mild flattening in the 10Y-20Y segment. The short end is moving faster than the long end.
commentaries.outlook: Expect continued downward pressure on the short end as excess liquidity persists. The long end needs a catalyst — either a clear signal from BB on further cuts or a shift in institutional appetite.
